docs.title: revolut-merchant-py
docs.text: A typed Python client for the Revolut Merchant API — online payments (orders, payments, refunds, customers, saved methods, subscriptions, payouts, disputes, report runs, locations) and webhooks — with both synchronous and asynchronous clients.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install revolut-merchant-py

The distribution is revolut-merchant-py; the import package is revolut.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
from revolut import RevolutMerchantClient

with RevolutMerchantClient(secret_key="sk_...", environment="sandbox") as client:
    order = client.orders.create(amount=1000, currency="GBP")  # 1000 = £10.00
    print(order.id, order.state, order.public_token)

See Usage for the full guide and API reference for every type.

docs.title: revolut.RevolutMerchantClient
docs.text:
Synchronous Revolut Merchant API client.

Example

client = RevolutMerchantClient(secret_key="sk_...", environment="sandbox") order = client.orders.create(amount=1000, currency="GBP")

docs.title: revolut.AsyncRevolutMerchantClient
docs.text:
Asynchronous Revolut Merchant API client.

Example

async with AsyncRevolutMerchantClient(secret_key="sk_...") as client: ... order = await client.orders.create(amount=1000, currency="GBP")

docs.title: revolut.verify_signature(raw_body, *, signature_header, timestamp_header, signing_secret, tolerance_seconds=DEFAULT_TOLERANCE_SECONDS, now=None)
docs.text:
Verify a webhook delivery and return its parsed payload.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default raw_body str | bytes

The exact request body as received (do not re-serialize).

required signature_header str

The Revolut-Signature header value.

required timestamp_header str

The Revolut-Request-Timestamp header (Unix ms).

required signing_secret str

The webhook's signing_secret (wsk_...).

required tolerance_seconds int

Reject deliveries older than this (replay protection).

DEFAULT_TOLERANCE_SECONDS now float | None

Current time in epoch seconds; defaults to :func:time.time.

None

Raises:

Type Description SignatureVerificationError

If the timestamp is stale or no signature matches.

docs.title: revolut.compute_signature(*, timestamp, raw_body, signing_secret)
docs.text: Return the expected v1=<hex> signature for a payload.

docs.title: revolut.Environment
docs.text:
Bases: str, Enum

Target Revolut environment.

docs.title: revolut.RetryConfig dataclass
docs.text: Controls automatic retries for transient failures.

docs.title: backoff_seconds(attempt, retry_after, rng=None)
docs.text:
Seconds to wait before the next attempt (0-indexed attempt).

When jitter > 0, up to jitter * base extra seconds are added (using rng, defaulting to :func:random.random) to de-correlate retries from concurrent clients.

docs.title: revolut.Money
docs.text:
Convenience helper to convert between major and minor currency units.

The Revolut API always works with integer minor units (e.g. 1099 for £10.99). These helpers make conversions explicit and currency-aware.

docs.title: from_major(value, currency) classmethod
docs.text: Build from a major-unit value, e.g. Money.from_major("10.99", "GBP").

docs.title: to_major()
docs.text: Return the major-unit value as a :class:~decimal.Decimal.

docs.title: revolut.Order
docs.text:
Bases: RevolutModel

A Merchant API order.

Amounts are integers in the currency's minor units. The public identifier is token; public_id is accepted for compatibility with the legacy create-order endpoint.

docs.title: public_token property
docs.text: The public identifier, preferring token over legacy public_id.

docs.title: revolut.Payment
docs.text:
Bases: RevolutModel

A single payment attempt against an order.

docs.title: revolut.PaymentMethod
docs.text:
Bases: RevolutModel

A payment method saved against a customer (tokenized).

docs.title: revolut.Customer
docs.text:
Bases: RevolutModel

A Merchant API customer.

docs.title: revolut.Subscription
docs.text:
Bases: RevolutModel

A recurring-payment subscription.

docs.title: revolut.Payout
docs.text:
Bases: RevolutModel

A settlement payout to the merchant's account.

docs.title: revolut.Location
docs.text:
Bases: RevolutModel

A merchant location / domain used for checkout.

docs.title: revolut.Webhook
docs.text:
Bases: RevolutModel

A configured webhook endpoint.

signing_secret (prefixed wsk_) is returned only on creation and on secret rotation; keep it to verify delivered payloads.

docs.title: revolut.WebhookEventPayload
docs.text:
Bases: RevolutModel

The JSON body Revolut POSTs to your webhook URL.

docs.title: revolut.RevolutError
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Base class for every error raised by this library.

docs.title: revolut.APIStatusError
docs.text:
Bases: RevolutError

Raised when the API returns a non-2xx HTTP status.

Attributes:

Name Type Description status_code

The HTTP status code.

code

The Revolut error code from the response body, if any.

request_id

The Revolut request id (from the X-Request-Id header).

body

The parsed JSON body, if any.

docs.title: Money
docs.text:
Amounts are integers in minor units (e.g. 1099 = £10.99).

from revolut import Money

Money.from_major("10.99", "GBP").amount   # 1099
Money(1234, "BHD").to_major()             # Decimal("1.234")

docs.title: Orders
docs.text:
order = client.orders.create(
    amount=5000, currency="EUR",
    capture_mode="manual",
    merchant_order_data={"reference": "INV-42"},
    idempotency_key="INV-42",
)
client.orders.capture(order.id, amount=5000)
client.orders.cancel(order.id)

for o in client.orders.iter(state="completed"):
    ...

docs.title: Refunds
docs.text: refund = client.refunds.create(order.id, amount=2500, idempotency_key="INV-42-r1")

docs.title: Customers & saved payment methods
docs.text:
customer = client.customers.create(email="a@b.com")
methods = client.payment_methods.list(customer.id, only_merchant=True)
client.payments.pay(order.id, payment_method_id=methods[0].id)

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text:
from revolut import verify_signature, SignatureVerificationError

event = verify_signature(
    raw_body=request.body,
    signature_header=request.headers["Revolut-Signature"],
    timestamp_header=request.headers["Revolut-Request-Timestamp"],
    signing_secret="wsk_...",
)

docs.title: Async
docs.text:
Every resource has an async twin via AsyncRevolutMerchantClient:

async with AsyncRevolutMerchantClient(secret_key="sk_...") as client:
    order = await client.orders.create(amount=1000, currency="GBP")
    async for o in client.orders.iter():
        ...

docs.title: Errors & retries
docs.text: Non-2xx responses raise typed RevolutError subclasses. Transient 429/5xx are retried with exponential backoff; tune with RetryConfig(max_retries=..., jitter=...).
